metadata.scope: code-side drift remaining after README/docs restructure
metadata.excludes: any further README rewrites; any docs-tree reorg
metadata.revision-note: v0.4 applies 5 user-requested refinements to v0.3: 1. Out-of-scope list expanded with the remaining D6 drifts: packages/core/src/config.ts defaultConfig, packages/db/src/schema/tables.ts TABLES_DDL table-name constants, apps/cli/src/yaml-io.ts .cognit/ templates. Each is a real duplication but a separate consolidation concern. Listed under out-of-scope for honest deferral. 2. AC28 replaced with a compile-time TypeScript assertion (new packages/db/src/event-schema-keys.ts). Old wording "module-load validation" implied runtime; new wording uses conditional types + module-level const that fails to compile if a key is missing or extra. No runtime cost. 3. Phase H verification replaced the fragile grep with the compile-time assertion re-run (machine-checkable) + a manual-review note for the hook-script side (the only remaining literal occurrences are intentional script templates, not duplicates). 4. Batch 3 split into 3A → 3B → 3C explicitly sequential. Each sub-batch's "parallel-safe: NO" annotation makes the no-parallelism contract unambiguous to the implementer agent. 5. Phase F scope expanded with regression tests for the two new hook scripts (gemini-post.sh, codex-pre.sh) and cognit env (the new subcommand from Phase B).

ac.item: All drift items cite file:line evidence.
No code modified.
Drift list bounded to actionable items.

phase.name: wrap-test-fixture-alignment
phase.problem: packages/wrap/test/index.test.ts:266 stamps schema_version=1.1.0 and asserts the old field name. Drift vs packages/wrap/src/index.ts:72 (WRAP_SCHEMA_VERSION="1.2.0") and the FLAT v1.2.0 envelope.

ac.item: Test name + assertion updated to `version: "1.2.0"`.
Test continues to assert envelope shape (FLAT, id, actor_name, actor_type).
pnpm typecheck passes.
pnpm --filter @cognit/wrap test passes.

phase.name: cognit-env-shell-subcommand
phase.problem: Docs (cli.md:94, hooks/README.md, getting-started.md) reference `eval "$(cognit init --shell)"` for exporting $COGNIT_INBOX. apps/cli/src/commands/init.ts has no --shell flag. Three users hit "unknown option". v0.1 proposed adding a --shell flag to init; v0.2 architecture review rejected — init without flags has side effects (creates .cognit/, DB), while --shell would have none. Confusing dual-purpose command. Split into a separate subcommand.
phase.design:
New subcommand: `cognit env --shell`. Prints an `eval`-able snippet of `export KEY="value"` lines for the env vars hook scripts need. Currently: `export COGNIT_INBOX=" /.cognit/inbox"`. Future additions (e.g. COGNIT_SESSION_ID, COGNIT_ACTOR_NAME) land in this command without touching init's bootstrap semantics.
Behaviour: - `cognit env --shell` → stdout = `export COGNIT_INBOX="/.cognit/inbox"` exit 0. No side effects. No DB, no .cognit/ writes. - `cognit env` (no flag) → prints a human-readable table of the same vars and their current values (read-only). - `cognit env KEY` (single arg) → prints just that value, one line. Used by scripts that want to compose. - Honours --root <path> (resolves project root the same way `cognit init` does).
Docs update (cli.md:94, hooks/README.md, getting-started.md): - Replace `eval "$(cognit init --shell)"` with `eval "$(cognit env --shell)"`. - Add a `cognit env` entry to docs/cli.md lifecycle table.

ac.item: apps/cli/src/commands/env.ts created; registers `env` subcommand on the Commander program.
`cognit env --shell` prints `export COGNIT_INBOX="/.cognit/inbox"` to stdout. Exit 0. No DB write, no .cognit/ creation.
`cognit env` (no flag) prints a readable table. Exit 0.
`cognit env COGNIT_INBOX` prints the value on one line. Exit 0.
All three forms honour --root <path> (path resolves from <path>, not cwd).
Bare `cognit init` behaviour UNCHANGED (still creates .cognit/, DB, cognit.yaml, .gitignore; idempotent).
docs/cli.md lifecycle table gains an `env` row.
docs/{cli.md,hooks/README.md,getting-started.md} replace all `cognit init --shell` references with `cognit env --shell`.
pnpm typecheck passes.
Manual smoke: `cd /tmp/test-project && eval "$(cognit env --shell)" && echo "$COGNIT_INBOX"` prints the project inbox.

phase.verification: pnpm typecheck && cd /tmp/test-project && eval "$(cognit env --shell)" && echo "$COGNIT_INBOX"
phase.risk: medium — new subcommand + 3-doc update. Must NOT regress init.

phase.name: missing-hook-scripts
phase.problem: docs/hooks/gemini-cli.md and docs/hooks/codex.md reference hooks/gemini-cli/gemini-post.sh and hooks/codex/codex-pre.sh. Neither file ships.

ac.item: hooks/gemini-cli/gemini-post.sh created. Mirrors cc-post.sh session-id resolution ($COGNIT_SESSION_ID → .cognit/current-session → placeholder) + atomic-write primitive (open wx → write → fsync → close → rename). actor_name="gemini-cli", source.tool="gemini-cli", source.command="AfterTool".
hooks/codex/codex-pre.sh created. Mirrors cc-pre.sh session-id resolution + known-files gate. actor_name="codex", type="hypothesis_created", title=tool, text="agent intends to <tool> <path>".
docs/hooks/gemini-cli.md: install command updated to reference the new file; "Phase G" framing removed.
docs/hooks/codex.md: install command updated to reference codex-pre.sh; "Phase G companion" framing removed.
bash -n parses both new scripts without error.
End-to-end smoke: each new script emits a valid v1.2.0 envelope under /tmp/cognit-smoke (the harness created in Phase F; before F exists, run manually with the same pattern used in prior rounds).

phase.verification: bash -n hooks/gemini-cli/gemini-post.sh hooks/codex/codex-pre.sh && manual smoke per script
phase.risk: medium — new shell scripts. Must match canonical session-id resolution order.

phase.name: theory-experiment-soft-deprecate
phase.problem: Code treats Theory + Experiment as first-class state types + CLI subcommands + reducer branches. README soft-deprecates. User said Theory is rarely used (twice). v0.2 chose option D1: soft-deprecate via warning + doc note.
phase.warning-suppression: Per-invocation check: if env var COGNIT_QUIET_DEPRECATIONS=1 is set, suppress the warning. Default (unset): emit warning once per process invocation (process-local flag, not persisted to disk; no on-disk state; no DB write). Suppressing prevents spam in scripts that loop over cognit theory / cognit experiment. Documented in docs/cli.md under the theory + experiment rows.

ac.item: docs/cli.md: theory + experiment entries marked "experimental" with the warning-suppression note (mention COGNIT_QUIET_DEPRECATIONS=1) and a one-line note ("rarely needed for canonical investigation flow; see README §What Cognit Stores").

apps/cli/src/commands/theory.ts: emits a stderr warning on first invocation per process unless COGNIT_QUIET_DEPRECATIONS=1. Warning text: `warning: cognit theory is rarely needed; most investigations use Observation → Finding → Hypothesis → Verification → Conclusion → Decision`. Set COGNIT_QUIET_DEPRECATIONS=1 to suppress.
apps/cli/src/commands/experiment.ts: same warning shape (text adapted: "cognit experiment is rarely needed…").

Warning does NOT block execution (exit 0 on success, 1 on real error). Warning emitted at most once per process invocation. Implementation: module-level `let warned = false` in a new apps/cli/src/commands/_deprecation.ts helper exported as `warnExperimentalOnce(label: string)`. Both theory.ts and experiment.ts import and call this helper; the flag is shared so two `cognit theory` invocations in the same process still emit exactly one warning each.
pnpm typecheck passes.

phase.verification: pnpm typecheck && cd /tmp/test-project && cognit theory create "x" 2>&1 | grep "warning:" && COGNIT_QUIET_DEPRECATIONS=1 cognit theory create "x" 2>&1 | grep -v "warning:"
phase.risk: low — doc text + one stderr line per process invocation.

phase.name: source-of-truth-consolidation-ts
phase.problem: Duplicated constants across packages and apps: - STATE_EVENT_TYPES Set at packages/core/src/reducer.ts:42-74 (canonical via switch exhaustiveness). - PAYLOAD_SCHEMAS_V1 keys at packages/db/src/event-schema.ts:256+ (second copy of the same event-type names). - WRAP_SCHEMA_VERSION at packages/wrap/src/index.ts:72 ("1.2.0"; also hardcoded in cc-post.sh:71, codex-post.sh:58, cc-pre.sh:84, gemini-hooks.json _doc, event-schema.ts:15). - VALID_ACTOR_TYPES redeclared in 9 CLI command files (append.ts, artifact.ts, experiment.ts, finding.ts, conclusion.ts, session.ts, constraint.ts, edge.ts, theory.ts). - WrapEnvelopeType subset at packages/wrap/src/index.ts:79-83 (only 4 types — different from full set). v0.1 proposed creating a NEW packages/core/src/event-types.ts as source of truth. v0.2 architecture reviewer REJECTED — that would create a THIRD copy of STATE_EVENT_TYPES. Correct direction: extract from reducer.ts (canonical via exhaustiveness check), have event-schema.ts import, CLI commands import VALID_ACTOR_TYPES from a single source.
phase.direction: 1. Create packages/core/src/event-types.ts by MOVING STATE_EVENT_TYPES + NON_STATE_EVENT_TYPES + ALL_KNOWN_TYPES from reducer.ts:42-88. Re-export from packages/core/src/index.ts. 2. packages/core/src/reducer.ts: import from ./event-types.js. 3. packages/db/src/event-schema.ts: PAYLOAD_SCHEMAS_V1 keys checked at compile time against the imported set (defence-in-depth; surfaces missing-type bugs at typecheck (compile-time assertion in event-schema-keys.ts)). 4. packages/wrap/src/index.ts: WrapEnvelopeType derives from a SUBTYPE of the imported set (intersection type or `Extract<>`). WRAP_SCHEMA_VERSION stays here but exported from core (single source for the version literal). 5. apps/cli/src/commands/*.ts: VALID_ACTOR_TYPES redeclared 9 times → replaced with a single import from a new packages/core/src/actor-types.ts (or reuse the ActorType union from @cognit/db).

ac.item: packages/core/src/event-types.ts created. STATE_EVENT_TYPES + NON_STATE_EVENT_TYPES + ALL_KNOWN_TYPES moved from reducer.ts. Exported.
packages/core/src/reducer.ts: imports event-type sets from ./event-types.js. Behaviour unchanged.

packages/db/src/event-schema-keys.ts (new file) provides a compile-time assertion that `keyof typeof PAYLOAD_SCHEMAS_V1` exactly equals `STATE_EVENT_TYPES` (subset + superset, no missing or extra keys). Implementation uses TypeScript conditional types and module-level `const _check: ... = true;` so a drift is a TypeScript compile error, not a runtime module-load throw. Files: imports STATE_EVENT_TYPES from `@cognit/core/event-types`, imports PAYLOAD_SCHEMAS_V1 from `./event-schema.js`. The file has no runtime effect (just the type assertions + a single export {}).

packages/wrap/src/index.ts: WrapEnvelopeType = Extract<StateEventType, "observation_recorded" | "verification_passed" | "verification_failed" | "verification_errored">. WRAP_SCHEMA_VERSION stays exported here; comment notes it must equal @cognit/db CURRENT_VERSION (a single source for the version literal is a follow-up; this phase leaves the duplication).
packages/core/src/actor-types.ts created. Exports ACTOR_TYPES tuple ["human", "worker", "system"] + ACTOR_TYPE union type. Re-exported from packages/core/src/index.ts.

apps/cli/src/commands/{append,artifact,conclusion,constraint,decision,edge,experiment,finding,hypothesis,observation,session,snapshot,theory,verification}.ts: VALID_ACTOR_TYPES replaced with `new Set(ACTOR_TYPES)` (single import). 14 files consolidated.
pnpm typecheck passes.
pnpm --filter @cognit/cli test passes (or manual smoke if no tests exist for these commands).

phase.verification: pnpm typecheck && grep -rn "VALID_ACTOR_TYPES" apps/cli/src/commands/ | wc -l → 0 (all imports, not redeclarations) && pnpm --filter @cognit/cli test 2>/dev/null || manual smoke
phase.risk: medium — ~20 modified files (14 CLI commands + 5 package files: reducer.ts, wrap/src/index.ts, event-schema.ts, packages/core/src/index.ts, packages/core/src/reducer.ts) + 3 new files (event-types.ts, actor-types.ts, event-schema-keys.ts). Mechanical rename for the CLI side; reducer extraction requires preserving exhaustiveness check.
phase.deferred: Hook-script side of the same consolidation (the literal "observation_recorded" in cc-post.sh:74 etc.) is NOT in this phase. v0.2 architecture reviewer noted: TS constants cannot cross the shell boundary without a codegen step. The hooks would need either a generate-hooks-consts.sh emit step (new tooling) or a runtime source (jq --arg with an env var fed by a parent process). Both are real scope. Deferred to a follow-up plan; this phase handles the TS-side consolidation only.

phase.name: tests-for-new-code
phase.problem: Code added/modified in prior rounds without tests: - packages/core/src/paths.ts (7 exports, 0 tests) - apps/cli/src/paths.ts shim (0 tests) - hooks/* shell scripts (only manual smoke in /tmp) - Phase E renames event-type + actor-type consolidation (need regression tests for the new symbol names)

ac.item: packages/core/test/paths.test.ts: covers findProjectRoot (hit, miss, stop at root), cognitDir, projectPaths (all keys present), isCognitProject, expandHome (~/, ~/x, absolute).

packages/core/test/event-types.test.ts: ALL_KNOWN_TYPES is the union of STATE_EVENT_TYPES + NON_STATE_EVENT_TYPES, with no overlap. STATE_EVENT_TYPES contains the documented types. Sample exhaustiveness: switch over STATE_EVENT_TYPES covers all members without `default` branch.
packages/core/test/actor-types.test.ts: ACTOR_TYPES has exactly ["human", "worker", "system"]. Round-trip a Set membership check.

apps/cli/test/paths.test.ts: uses ESM `import` (project is ESM, not CJS) from @cognit/core/paths via the shim; verifies the shim re-exports every symbol (`import { findProjectRoot, cognitDir, projectPaths, COGNIT_SUBDIRS, COGNIT_FILES, ProjectPaths, isCognitProject, expandHome } from "@cognit/core/paths"`; assert each is a function or expected value).

hooks/test/test-atomic-write.sh: runs the python atomic-write primitive against /tmp/cognit-smoke, asserts file exists with mode 0o600, no .tmp leftover, JSON content parses as a valid v1.2.0 envelope.
hooks/test/test-cc-post.sh: invokes cc-post.sh with a mocked stdin JSON, asserts the resulting envelope file has version=1.2.0, actor_name=claude-code, id present, mode 0o600.
pnpm test (or equivalent) runs all suites and passes.

apps/cli/test/env.test.ts: covers `cognit env --shell` (stdout is `export COGNIT_INBOX="/.cognit/inbox"`, exit 0, no DB write, no .cognit/ creation), `cognit env` (no flag, readable table, exit 0), `cognit env COGNIT_INBOX` (one-line value, exit 0), and --root <path> interaction (output reflects <path>, not cwd).

hooks/test/test-gemini-post.sh: invokes the new gemini-post.sh with a mocked stdin JSON (matching Gemini's AfterTool shape), asserts the resulting envelope file has version=1.2.0, actor_name=gemini-cli, source.tool=gemini-cli, source.command=AfterTool, id present, mode 0o600.

hooks/test/test-codex-pre.sh: invokes the new codex-pre.sh with a mocked stdin JSON (Codex PreToolUse shape, file_path field set), asserts the resulting envelope file has version=1.2.0, actor_name=codex, type=hypothesis_created, title=<tool name>, text contains the file path, mode 0o600.

phase.verification: pnpm test && bash hooks/test/test-atomic-write.sh && bash hooks/test/test-cc-post.sh && bash hooks/test/test-gemini-post.sh && bash hooks/test/test-codex-pre.sh && pnpm --filter @cognit/cli test 2>/dev/null (includes env.test.ts) || manual smoke for env
phase.note: v0.1 put F in batch 4 (after E). v0.2 architecture reviewer flagged: tests should validate behaviour AND the new symbol names together, otherwise the tests are asserting the new constant's name, not its behaviour. Resolution: F runs IMMEDIATELY after E in the same batch (batch 3 below). F's per-test ACs reference the symbols E introduces, so F is co-dependent on E.

phase.name: stale-phase-comments
phase.problem: 8 stale references to "Phase E" / "Phase G" in hooks/ and docs/hooks/. Project is past those phases.

ac.item: Zero remaining "Phase E" or "Phase G" references in hooks/ and docs/hooks/.
Replacements describe current state (e.g. "Pre-tool hook — emits hypothesis_created before each Claude Code tool call" instead of "deferred to Phase G").

phase.name: cross-cutting-verification
phase.problem: Seven phases of disjoint edits. Cross-check prevents drift from re-appearing.

scope.verify: grep -rn "VALID_ACTOR_TYPES" apps/cli/src/commands/ | should only show imports, not redeclarations

Two checks: (a) machine-checkable: `pnpm typecheck` re-runs the compile-time assertion from packages/db/src/event-schema-keys.ts (AC28). If the assertion holds, the canonical registry keys exactly match STATE_EVENT_TYPES — no orphaned literal can exist in any TS file that imports from `@cognit/core/event-types`. (b) manual review: verifier reads `grep -rn "observation_recorded\|finding_created\|hypothesis_created" hooks/` and confirms each hit is a script-internal jq template (e.g. `jq -n --arg type "observation_recorded" ...`), not a duplicate of the canonical constant. Documented as "manual review" because grep output is not machine-checkable but the judgement is straightforward.
grep -rn "cognit init --shell" docs/ → 0 hits (should be `cognit env --shell`)
bash -n on every hook script → exit 0

ac.item: All Phase A-G verifications re-run and pass.
No new drift introduced by this round.
Final review agent confirms the working tree is "ready to commit" or surfaces remaining drift as a follow-up task.

dependencies.block: D → E (D modifies theory.ts + experiment.ts; E then modifies them again for VALID_ACTOR_TYPES consolidation)
E → F (F tests reference symbols E introduces)

out-of-scope.item: Any further README rewrites (already restructured 3 rounds).
Hard-removing Theory/Experiment from code (option D2; needs explicit user approval).
CI wiring for hooks/test/*.sh (Phase F creates the tests; integration is its own plan).
Hook-script side of source-of-truth (TS constants don't cross shell boundary without codegen; deferred).
Single source of truth for the schema-version literal "1.2.0" (still declared in 4+ places after Phase E; deferred).
Reversing apps/cli/src/commands/{theory,experiment}.ts rename — Phase D keeps the names.

D6 remainder (deferred per user direction — each is a real drift but a distinct consolidation concern; belongs in a follow-up round): - packages/core/src/config.ts `defaultConfig` consolidation. Some CLI commands still hard-code defaults (e.g. `project.name` fallback). Migration requires a config- resolution pass that touches every CLI command — out of scope for the source-of-truth round (which targets event-type + actor-type literals only). - packages/db/src/schema/tables.ts `TABLES_DDL` table-name constants. Multiple CLI files query by raw string (`"events"`, `"sessions"`, etc.); a `TABLES.EVENTS` constant would consolidate them. Distinct from Phase E because the consumers are SQL-builder call sites, not envelope-shape producers. - apps/cli/src/yaml-io.ts .cognit/ templates (`writeCognitYaml`, `writeCognitGitignore`). The init command has its own copy of the templates; future moves to a single config-writer helper belong with the config consolidation round, not the source-of-truth round.

review-gate.criterion: ≥3 reviewer agents approve v0.5 before any phase executes.

reviewers.reviewer: Direction-of-effort (Phase E consolidation, Phase B subcommand split, Phase D warning spec).

review-gate.fail-policy: If 2+ reviewers reject, revise to v0.6 and re-review.
